revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:生國魂祭]]
'''Ikutama Festival''' (生國魂祭, Ikutama Matsuri) is a festival held annually at {{ill|Ikukunitama Shrine|en|Ikukunitama Shrine|qid=Q11574477|lt=Ikukunitama Shrine|1=Ikukunitama Shrine}} in Tennoji Ward, Osaka City. The festival occurs from July 11th to July 12th and is considered one of the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|ja|大阪三大夏祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}.
== Overview == While {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|Tenjin Matsuri|qid=Q5371810|lt=Tenjin Matsuri|1=Tenjin Matsuri}} of {{ill|Osaka Tenmangū|en|Osaka Tenmangū|qid=Q385793|lt=Osaka Tenmangu|1=Osaka Tenmangū}} is famous for its water-based procession known as Funatogyo, Ikutama Festival is known for its land-based procession called Rikutogyo<ref name="tennoji17">[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tennoji/cmsfiles/contents/0000404/404034/tennoji17-07_p02.pdf Tennoji Ward Public Relations, July 1, 2017, No.254]</ref>. This procession symbolizes returning the deity's sacred object to Osaka Castle, the original location of Ikukunitama Shrine<ref name="tennoji17"/>.
; July 11 (Yoimiya - Festival Eve) * Children's Taiko Performance *: Performed by students of Osaka Municipal Ikutama Elementary School<ref name="tennoji17" />. * Parade of Gold and Silver Mikoshi *: Participants include local men from Tennoji Ward and students from {{ill|Uenomiya High School|ja|上宮高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uenomiya High School|1=Uenomiya High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358787}} and {{ill|Seifu High School|ja|清風高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seifu High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 清風中学校・高等学校|1=Seifu Junior and Senior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7583155}}<ref name="tennoji17" />.
; July 12 (Main Festival) * Main Festival Ceremony (Honkō-sai) *: A ceremony held at Ikukunitama Shrine before the procession begins<ref name="nnn2021" />. * Rikutogyo Procession *: A two-hour procession from Ikukunitama Shrine to Osaka Castle, led by Makura-Daiko, a taiko drum historically offered by Toyotomi Hideyoshi<ref name="tennoji17" /><ref name="book" />. * Motomiya Churen Ceremony *: Conducted after the procession at the former shrine location in Osaka Castle Park's Otemae lawn area, concluded with the traditional "Ikukunitama-jime" hand-clapping ritual<ref name="nnn2021" />.
== History == The Rikutogyo procession historically involved over 2,000 participants but was discontinued after WWII due to the loss of ritual items during the Osaka Air Raids<ref name="tennoji17" />.
In 1990, the festival partially resumed with simplified mikoshi carried by vehicles<ref name="tennoji17" />. A full restoration occurred in 2014, thanks to community donations for a new "Go-hōren" (sacred palanquin), reviving the original procession<ref name="tennoji17" />.
From 2020 to 2022, due to COVID-19 prevention measures, the procession was canceled, and only essential rituals were performed<ref name="nnn2021">[https://www.nnn.co.jp/dainichi/news/210713/20210713020.html Osaka Nichi Nichi Shimbun, July 13, 2021]</ref><ref name="book">[https://www.ikutamajinja.jp/pdf/book_20200629.pdf Announcement of the cancellation of the Ikutama Festival procession, April 24, 2021]</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|生國魂祭|version=101780924|comment=Imported full ja history}}
